text: Major corporations are ordering full-time office returns, sparking fierce backlash, AP News reports.
Harvard Business Review found hybrid models boost productivity by fifteen percent, contradicting several CEOs.
Gallup says sixty percent of workers prefer hybrid and would consider quitting.
McKinsey estimates remote work saves ten thousand dollars per employee annually.
The debate shows no signs of settling down.
The data overwhelmingly favors hybrid work on both productivity and retention.
Companies that resist flexibility risk losing top talent to competitors who embrace it.
